packet.criterion_text: Objective severity trend: PASI, BSA, and/or IGA show a directional change across visits.

evidence.quote: Severity scoring today: PASI 6.1, BSA 8%, IGA 2
PASI 6.3 today (6.1 on 2026-02-02 — essentially flat, within measurement noise). BSA 8%. IGA 2.
Few scattered erythematous papules on the antecubital arm, limited extent
Confluent erythematous plaques with overlying scale over the arm, extensive

packet.reasoning: PASI rose from 6.1 (2026-02-02) and 6.3 (2026-03-16) to 14.2 (2026-05-10) and 16.8 (2026-06-29), a rise far exceeding the 2-point threshold; BSA rose from 8% to 18% and IGA stepped up from 2 to 3. The photographic progression from limited papules to extensive confluent plaques corroborates the objective worsening. Note: whether this measured worsening is attributable to disease activity rather than a treatment gap is addressed (and conceded) under criterion 5.

packet.criterion_text: Symptom trajectory, including nail and joint involvement, shows a directional change.

evidence.quote: onycholysis noted on two fingernails (right index and right middle), with distal separation and slight yellowish discoloration
Nail plate pitting with mild subungual discoloration and ridging
Erythematous scaly patch with scale along the frontal scalp hairline, localized
Thick adherent scale over erythematous frontal scalp plaques, moderate to extensive
Grouped erythematous papules and small plaques over the arm, mild to moderate extent
Confluent erythematous plaques with overlying scale over the arm, extensive

packet.reasoning: New nail involvement (onycholysis on two fingernails plus photographed pitting) appears on 2026-05-10, and photographs document scalp disease at 2026-05-10 that progresses from localized to moderate-to-extensive by 2026-06-29, alongside arm lesions progressing from mild-to-moderate papules to extensive confluent plaques. Together these show a worsening symptom trajectory including new nail and scalp sites. No joint symptoms are documented in the candidates. Note: all documented symptom progression falls within the same interval as the biologic coverage gap conceded under criterion 5.
packet.gap: No visit after May 10 re-examined or re-documented the fingernail changes that were first noted then.

packet.criterion_text: DLQI (Dermatology Life Quality Index) trend shows a directional change.

evidence.quote: DLQI 7, driven by residual itch and self-consciousness at the gym.
DLQI 15 today (was 8), with high scores on symptoms, feelings, and work domains.
DLQI 17. Compared with 2026-05-10 (PASI 14.2, DLQI 15), objective and quality-of-life measures are both worse.

packet.reasoning: DLQI rose from 7 (2026-02-02) and 8 (2026-03-16) to 15 (2026-05-10) and 17 (2026-06-29). The jump from 8 to 15 exceeds the 4-point threshold for meaningful worsening, and the subsequent rise to 17 confirms a sustained upward trend.

packet.criterion_text: Treatment-response adequacy: the current regimen has been given an adequate, adherent trial.

evidence.quote: Adherence and pharmacy fill detail deferred to today's separate review by clinic staff (filed separately).

packet.reasoning: The chart itself defers adherence and pharmacy fill details to a separately filed review that is not among the provided evidence, so the trial's adequacy cannot be established from chart quotes. Moreover, the reviewer's pharmacy dispense records (MD-004, MD-005) indicate the last adalimumab dispense was 2026-03-16 with a 28-day supply and no subsequent refill, meaning the regimen was not taken as scheduled after 2026-04-13. On both grounds, an adequate, adherent trial cannot be affirmed. Documentation that would settle this: a completed adherence/pharmacy-fill review confirming scheduled dosing, or confirmation of resumed adherence with sufficient duration to judge response.
packet.gap: The June 29 visit deferred the adherence and pharmacy-fill review to a separate document that is not in the chart provided; dispense records indicate no adalimumab refill after 2026-03-16.

packet.criterion_text: Worsening cannot be attributed to disease if a confounder (non-adherence, infection, steroid-withdrawal or seasonal flare) is unaddressed.
packet.reasoning: Conceded. The reviewer's pharmacy dispense records (MD-004: last adalimumab dispense 2026-03-16, 28-day supply, coverage ended 2026-04-13; MD-005: no adalimumab refill thereafter) establish a biologic non-adherence confounder that the chart cannot rebut: a targeted search returned no chart evidence that the most recent deterioration began before the coverage lapse in mid-April, and every measurement documenting the flare (2026-05-10 and 2026-06-29) postdates the lapse. The rubric requires precedence for the most recent worsening interval, and that cannot be shown, so the progression cannot be separated from the treatment gap. Documentation that would settle this: (a) dated chart evidence that the deterioration started before biologic coverage lapsed on 2026-04-13, or (b) evidence that the severity rise persisted during a period of confirmed adherence — i.e., resume the biologic, confirm adherence via fills/administration records, and reassess severity after a period of resumed, confirmed adherence.
packet.gap: No chart documentation dates the onset of the latest worsening before the 2026-04-13 biologic coverage lapse, and no severity measurements exist during a period of confirmed adherence.

challenges.challenge_reason: We checked the pharmacy records: the patient's last adalimumab fill was March 16 with a 28-day supply, so coverage ran out April 13 and was never refilled. Every measurement showing the flare — May 10 and June 29 — came after the biologic lapsed, so the worsening can't be pinned on the disease itself until that treatment gap is dealt with.
challenges.rubric_quote: Worsening cannot be attributed to disease if a confounder (non-adherence, infection, steroid-withdrawal or seasonal flare) is unaddressed.
challenges.what_would_satisfy: We'd need documentation that the deterioration started before the biologic coverage lapsed in mid-April, or evidence the severity rise persisted during a period of confirmed adherence — otherwise a concession that the progression can't be separated from the treatment gap.

external_evidence.detail: adalimumab 40 mg SC q2wk, last dispense 2026-03-16, 28-day supply; coverage ended 2026-04-13; no subsequent adalimumab fill before the 2026-05-10 and 2026-06-29 visits documenting the latest worsening
only later fill of any kind is clobetasol ointment on 2026-06-15; no adalimumab refill after 2026-03-16

attempts: confounder missed doses infection steroid seasonal

patient_message: Hi, this is a note from your skin care team. Your psoriasis looks more active, but it seems some doses of your adalimumab shots may have been missed, so we can't tell the full story yet. Please restart your adalimumab as prescribed, and reply to let us know about when the flare-up began. We're happy to help if getting the medicine is a problem. — your dermatology care team
